danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based enemy entities in overworld and dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player; encounter risk increases in certain regions

danger_signals.visual_form: NPCs mention barbarians; likely visual presence in northern regions
danger_signals.meaning: Kingdom-wide political instability; external military threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Dragon sprite in cave on eastern edge of northern mountains
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger boss encounter; legendary threat to realm
Entering triggers permanent world state change (unleashes ghouls); increases difficulty significantly

danger_signals.visual_form: World color palette gradually shifts from day to night
danger_signals.meaning: Time-of-day state change; likely increased danger or NPC behavioral shifts

safety_signals.meaning: Safe rest point; restores health/status; resets NPC memory (crime forgiveness)

safety_signals.visual_form: Clustered buildings and NPCs in defined areas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone with quest-givers and services available
Increased safety; NPC availability for quests and interaction

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Settlements, inns, quest-givers representing ducal/baronial power structures

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Barbarian enemy sprites; distinct from civilian sprites
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in northern regions; mentioned by NPCs as invasion threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dragons, ghouls, generic monsters with distinct sprite designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Catacombs (ghoul stronghold), northern cave (dragon lair), scattered wilderness encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dukes and barons present as NPCs; unsettled atmosphere in settlements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs discuss conspiracy; quest-givers task player with unraveling power dynamics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPCs dialogue references; likely enemy patrols in northern regions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs warn of danger; possible increased barbarian enemy encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monster sprites distributed across overworld and dungeons
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Random monster encounters; combat encounters scale across map

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Catacomb structure visible but ghouls contained
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No catacomb ghoul encounters in world; normal difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual indication of released ghouls (sprite presence change or environmental shift)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ghouls roam world; dramatic difficulty increase; world becomes significantly more dangerous

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright color palette; clear visibility; animated water visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs active; quest availability; normal encounter rates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark color palette; reduced visual clarity; world becomes darker
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC behavior changes; likely reduced quest availability or altered encounter types

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Forest barrier on map; discovery reveals city structure behind forest
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to new NPCs; new quests and resources available upon discovery

environmental_storytelling.example: Serfs mention dragon in north, guiding player toward northern mountains where dragon actually sleeps in cave
Visible towns and villages communicate safe zones; inn structures identify rest points
Catacombs visual presence establishes dangerous lore; NPCs warn of consequences; entering triggers world transformation
Forest barrier hides Treela; mountain range contains dragon; regional distribution of threats across 13,000-screen map
Massive open world (13,000 screens) without loading conveys kingdom scope and complexity
Day-night transition visually marks time passage and associated danger/safety shifts

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remember transgression until player rests at inn; inn stay triggers memory reset

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Quest-critical NPCs (dukes, barons, magical item holders)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Provide quests and items; essential to game progression
Permanent loss; can cause unwinnable game state
Remain in catacomb; no world presence
Roam overworld; dramatically increase world danger; world difficulty spikes
Presence in northern regions; NPCs warn of threat; combat encounters likely
Visual sprite representation shows selected class archetype; different combat/quest engagement

notes: Times of Lore uses classic 8-bit RPG visual language with overhead map perspective. World-state communication relies heavily on: (1) NPC dialogue to establish political/danger context; (2) sprite-based entity presence to indicate threat level; (3) color cycling for day/night passage; (4) environmental structure (towns, dungeons, forests) to guide player navigation and establish safety/danger zones. The 13,000-screen persistent world without loading creates continuity of place that reinforces political and geographical world-state. Consequences system (NPCs remembering crimes until inn reset; unwinnable states from killing quest-givers; permanent world change from catacomb breach) embeds behavioral communication of player actions into world state. Hidden city behind forest teaches players to explore environmental boundaries as narrative content. This is foundational design for the later Ultima and Diablo lineages that emphasized environmental storytelling and persistent world consequence.
